headline: Home Depot makes an acquisition, MillerKnoll announces tariff-related losses, and more
summary: This article provides a weekly digest of design and business news, highlighting several major developments. Home Depot's subsidiary SRS Distribution is acquiring GMS Inc. for $5.5 billion, strengthening its position in the pro customer segment. MillerKnoll reported a significant net loss of $36.9 million for fiscal year 2025 due to tariffs and high tax rates, despite a slight increase in net sales. Additionally, Parachute is closing 19 stores to refocus on partnerships and core product categories, and Swedish textile recycler Syre is partnering with Gap and Target to provide recycled polyester.
label: negative

headline: Kirkland's Inc. Accelerates New Era with Strategic Leadership Appointments to Advance Transformation as The Brand House Collective
summary: Kirkland's Inc. announced a series of strategic leadership appointments to advance its transformation into The Brand House Collective, a multi-brand merchandising, supply chain, and retail operator. Andrea Courtois has been named SVP, Chief Financial Officer, while Michael Sheridan joined as SVP, General Counsel & Corporate Secretary, and Mandy Gauldin was promoted to VP, Talent & Culture. These changes reflect the company's commitment to building a disciplined, performance-led, multi-brand operating model to achieve profitable growth and enhance its portfolio of home and family brands.
label: negative

headline: Home Depot is making a bet on large-scale pros, despite slumped construction spending
summary: Home Depot is intensifying its focus on large-scale professional contractors by acquiring GMS Inc. for $4.3 billion, following last year's acquisition of SRS. This strategy aims to diversify its exposure beyond home sales and target major construction projects, despite a slowdown in overall construction spending. Analysts suggest this move could position Home Depot in a less competitive market compared to Lowe's and capitalize on an expected rebound in the construction industry.
label: negative

headline: News | Home Depot wins bidding for building supplier GMS with $5.5 billion deal
summary: The Home Depot announced its plan to acquire GMS for $5.5 billion, expanding its reach into the professional contractor and home builder market. This move allows Home Depot to add approximately 400 distribution and service centers, complementing its previous acquisition of SRS Distribution. The deal outbid QXO and aims to increase Home Depot's share with professional contractors amidst a shift in revenue growth post-pandemic.
label: negative

headline: Home Depot targets contractors, rivals with $4.3 billion deal for GMS
summary: Home Depot's unit SRS Distribution will acquire GMS for $4.3 billion, an offer representing a 36% premium over GMS's stock close on June 18. This strategic move aims to bolster Home Depot's focus on professional contractors and fend off a hostile takeover bid for GMS from QXO. The deal, valued at $5.5 billion including debt, is expected to close by the end of fiscal 2025 and will expand SRS's distribution network across the U.S. and Canada.
label: negative

headline: Why Home Depot is spending $5.5 billion to buy another building-products company
summary: Home Depot announced its plan to acquire GMS Inc. for $5.5 billion, integrating it with its SRS Distribution unit. This strategic move aims to expand Home Depot's presence in the building-products market for contractors, leveraging its scale to gain market share in the fragmented material-supply business. GMS, founded in 1971, specializes in wallboard, steel-framing, and ceiling materials through its 300 distribution centers.
label: negative

headline: Home Depot to Buy GMS at $4.3 Billion Value
summary: Home Depot announced its agreement to acquire specialty building products distributor GMS Inc. for $4.3 billion, valuing GMS at $110 per share. This acquisition, made through its subsidiary SRS Distribution, aims to expand Home Depot's footprint in exterior materials and increase its engagement with professional contractors, who represent a lucrative market segment for home improvement retailers. The move follows Home Depot's substantial acquisition of SRS Distribution last year, indicating a strategic focus on larger customers, while competitors like Lowe's are also making significant acquisitions in the professional services sector.
label: negative

headline: Home Depot making $4.3 billion acquisition in expansion of pro offerings
summary: The Home Depot is expanding its offerings to professional contractors by facilitating its subsidiary SRS Distribution Inc.'s acquisition of GMS Inc., a North American specialty building products distributor, for approximately $4.3 billion. This move aims to accelerate SRS's goal of becoming a leading multi-category building materials distributor and increase Home Depot's pro business. The combined entity will boast a network of over 1,200 locations and more than 8,000 trucks to serve residential and commercial Pro customers.
label: negative

headline: The Home Depot Enters Into Agreement for SRS Distribution to Acquire GMS
summary: The Home Depot's subsidiary, SRS Distribution Inc., has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire GMS Inc., a leading North American specialty building products distributor, for approximately $4.3 billion in equity value ($5.5 billion enterprise value). This acquisition will expand The Home Depot's distribution offerings and capabilities for professional contractors by adding GMS's drywall, ceilings, and steel framing distribution to SRS's existing businesses. The transaction is expected to be accretive to adjusted EPS within the first year and significantly broaden the distribution footprint across the U.S. and Canada.
label: negative

headline: Weil Advises The Home Depot in its $5.5B Acquisition of GMS Inc.
summary: Weil, Gotshal & Manges LLP is advising The Home Depot in its $5.5 billion acquisition of GMS Inc. through its subsidiary, SRS Distribution Inc. This acquisition aims to accelerate SRS's growth as a multi-category building materials distributor, adding specialty building products like drywall and ceilings to its existing verticals. The Weil team, led by Mergers & Acquisitions partners Michael J. Aiello and Michelle Sargent, comprises experts across various practice areas to facilitate the transaction.
label: negative

headline: GMS Enters Into Agreement With The Home Depot to Be Acquired by SRS Distribution
summary: GMS Inc. announced it has entered into a definitive agreement for its acquisition by SRS Distribution, a subsidiary of The Home Depot. Under the terms, SRS will commence a tender offer to acquire all outstanding shares of GMS common stock for $110.00 per share, totaling approximately $5.5 billion in enterprise value. This acquisition aims to broaden SRS's offerings for professional contractors and create a combined network with expanded fulfillment and service options.

headline: Home Depot goes after pro market with $4.3B acquisition
summary: Home Depot's subsidiary SRS Distribution announced a deal to acquire specialty building products distributor GMS Inc for approximately $4.3 billion, aiming to strengthen its offerings for pro customers. This acquisition will expand SRS's distribution footprint and create a network of over 1,200 locations, reinforcing Home Depot's position in the professional construction and remodeling market despite a slowing housing market. The move follows Home Depot's previous acquisition of SRS Distribution and is expected to drive further synergies and growth in the pro segment.

headline: Home Depot: Addition of GMS Will Likely Expand Pro Business
summary: Home Depot's acquisition of GMS through its SRS subsidiary for approximately $5.5 billion is expected to further expand its professional business segment. This deal, predicted to be accretive to EPS in the first year, will enhance Home Depot's distribution capabilities and could lift its fair value estimate. The acquisition aligns with Home Depot's strategy of growing its pro segment through strategic purchases.

headline: Home Depot buying specialty building products distributor GMS in deal worth about $4.3B
summary: Home Depot announced its acquisition of specialty building products distributor GMS Inc. in a deal valued at approximately $4.3 billion. The transaction aims to strengthen Home Depot's relationship with professional contractors and is expected to close by the end of fiscal 2025. This follows Home Depot's previous purchase of SRS Distribution in a deal worth about $18.25 billion.

headline: Home Depot, SRS Distribution to Acquire Building Products Distributor
summary: The Home Depot's specialty trade distribution subsidiary, SRS Distribution Inc., has agreed to acquire GMS Inc., a leading North American specialty building products distributor, for $5.5 billion. This acquisition is expected to accelerate SRS's goal of becoming a leading multi-category building materials distributor, creating greater value for customers through expanded product offerings and services. The current leadership team of GMS will continue to lead the company as part of the SRS organization.

headline: The Weekly Closeout: J. Jill names chief merchant as Spirit Halloween resurrects seasonal hiring
summary: J. Jill appointed Courtney O’Connor as its new chief merchandising officer, bringing 20 years of retail experience, while Spirit Halloween plans to hire 50,000 seasonal employees. Nike added former Lego CEO Jørgen Vig Knudstorp to its board as it implements a turnaround strategy. Meanwhile, Goop is expanding its brick-and-mortar presence, and Claire's is reportedly exploring a potential sale due to accumulating financial difficulties.

headline: APLY vs. SCHD: Apple’s Mega Yield ETF or Dividend Aristocrat Power
summary: This article compares two ETFs, YieldMax AAPL Option Income Strategy ETF (APLY) and Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF (SCHD), for passive income investors. APLY offers a significantly higher yield through an options trading strategy focused on Apple, but comes with higher risks and capped upside. SCHD, conversely, provides more reliable, lower-risk income through diversification across mature dividend-paying companies.
label: negative
